description: A Web Administrator needs to track active web connections on the local server to troubleshoot exhaustion issues. A script has been placed at ~/Desktop/check_web_connections.sh. In OpManager (http://localhost:8060, admin/Admin@123), do the following: 1) Create a new CLI/SSH Credential named 'Local-SSH-Service' with username 'ga', password 'password123', and Command Prompt '$'. 2) Create a new Script Template named 'Web-Active-Connections' that uses this script, polls every 5 minutes, expects numeric output, and triggers a Critical alarm if the value is > 500. 3) Associate this new script monitor with the localhost device (127.0.0.1) using the 'Local-SSH-Service' credential. Save all configurations.
